title: China-to-Bhutan Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC - IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-Series Evidence for Bhutan PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Bhutan Standards Bureau (BSB) is the national standards body, and Bhutan PV inverter projects should be checked for current BSB adoption or project-owner acceptance of IEC EMC standards. For grid-connected PV inverters, IEC 62920 is the dedicated EMC standard for photovoltaic power conversion equipment, and IEC 61000-series tests address emissions, immunity, harmonics, and power-quality compatibility. BEA, BPC, lenders, EPCs, or owners may require IEC EMC evidence as part of the grid-connection and technical submittal package.
target_standards: IEC 62920 - Photovoltaic power generating systems - EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series - Electromagnetic compatibility test methods, limits, emissions, immunity, and harmonic current requirements
Bhutan Standards Bureau - national standards adoption and conformity-assessment context
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence is commonly generated under GB/T 37408-2021 and related domestic test methods at CNAS-accredited laboratories. While China domestic test work may use IEC 61000-family methods conceptually, a GB/T 37408 EMC certificate does not automatically prove compliance with IEC 62920 or with any BSB, BEA, BPC, lender, owner, or EPC requirement for Bhutan. Reports should also be checked for the supplied model, power rating, firmware configuration, and 230/400 V use case.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 - 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) - includes EMC-related requirements
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese EMC certificates based solely on GB/T 37408-2021 are not automatically accepted as IEC 62920 or IEC 61000-series evidence for Bhutan projects. Exporters should prepare IEC 62920 EMC reports and applicable IEC 61000 harmonic, emission, and immunity evidence from an accredited laboratory, mapped to the exact inverter model and configuration. Where EMC or harmonic results depend on grid voltage or operating mode, confirm whether Bhutan's 230/400 V at 50 Hz conditions require additional evaluation.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent; mandatory in practice where BSB-adopted standards, BEA/BPC review, lender requirements, owner specifications, or EPC procurement documents require IEC EMC evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or project-driven for China domestic certification; not a substitute for Bhutan project EMC acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 37408-2021 EMC evidence should not be treated as automatic IEC 62920 or IEC 61000 compliance for Bhutan. Prepare IEC EMC reports for the specific inverter model and confirm with BSB, BEA, BPC, the EPC, or the owner whether Bhutan's 230/400 V at 50 Hz operating conditions require supplementary testing.

rows.requirement: Bhutan Grid Connection - IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 Evidence and 50 Hz / 230 V / 400 V Settings
rows.target_requirement: Bhutan's electricity sector is regulated by the Bhutan Electricity Authority (BEA), with Bhutan Power Corporation (BPC) acting as the principal utility and distribution grid operator. For grid-connected solar PV inverters, project approval is expected to focus on utility interconnection, anti-islanding, voltage and frequency protection, and compatibility with Bhutan's 50 Hz, 230/400 V low-voltage supply. IEC 62116 anti-islanding and IEC 61727 photovoltaic utility-interface evidence should be prepared for BEA, BPC, and project-owner review. Bhutan is a Himalayan, hydro-dominant system with more than 99% hydro generation and interconnection to India, so project-specific protection coordination and grid-code settings should be confirmed with BPC before commissioning.
target_standards: IEC 62116 - Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters - Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 - Photovoltaic systems - Characteristics of the utility interface
Bhutan Electricity Authority - electricity-sector regulatory requirements and licensing framework
Bhutan Power Corporation - project-level grid-connection and distribution network conditions
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 for photovoltaic power stations, NB/T 32004 for grid-connected inverter technical specifications, GB/T 37408-2021 inverter technical requirements, and domestic anti-islanding reports. These documents are based on China grid conditions and do not automatically satisfy BEA, BPC, or project-owner requirements in Bhutan. The critical parameter distinction is that Bhutan uses the same 50 Hz frequency as China, but its nominal low-voltage supply is 230/400 V, while China uses 220/380 V under GB/T 156.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 - 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004 - 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 - 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese GB/T and NB/T grid-connection documents should be treated as domestic baseline evidence only. Exporters should prepare IEC 62116 anti-islanding and IEC 61727 utility-interface reports for the exact inverter model or family, document firmware protection thresholds for Bhutan's 230/400 V, 50 Hz grid, and confirm BPC project-specific protection settings. Do not state that Bhutan voltage matches China: the frequency is the same at 50 Hz, but the nominal voltage differs from China's 220/380 V. For Himalayan sites, verify altitude-related thermal derating, creepage and clearance assumptions, and enclosure performance.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for any BPC-connected solar PV project where BEA, BPC, project owner, lender, or EPC technical review requires grid-interface and anti-islanding evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China domestic grid-connection baselines but is not automatically accepted for Bhutan BEA/BPC project approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat China GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, and GB/T 37408-2021 reports as non-transferable baseline evidence for Bhutan. Prepare IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 evidence and validate firmware against Bhutan's 230/400 V at 50 Hz grid before BPC connection review. Confirm current BEA rules and BPC project-specific protection settings directly before shipment or commissioning.

rows.requirement: Bhutan Project Approval - BEA / BPC Technical Review and High-Altitude Site Conditions
rows.target_requirement: Bhutan solar PV projects should be reviewed against the applicable BEA regulatory framework, BPC interconnection conditions, and project-owner technical specifications. The market is small, hydro-dominant, mountainous, and connected with India, so equipment acceptance is often project-specific rather than a simple national label check. Inverter submissions should include model identification, IEC safety and grid-interface reports, firmware setting sheets, protection coordination data, site altitude and ambient-temperature derating evidence, and installation/commissioning documentation acceptable to the EPC, owner, BPC, and BEA where applicable.
target_standards: Bhutan Electricity Authority - regulatory oversight for electricity supply and power-sector approvals
Bhutan Power Corporation - grid-connection, distribution network, metering, and commissioning requirements
IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 - commonly expected grid-interface evidence for utility-interconnected PV inverters
IEC 62109-1/-2 - commonly expected inverter safety evidence
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese domestic distributed PV approvals and grid-connection documentation are designed for China grid operators and Chinese voltage settings. They can support a technical file, but they do not replace Bhutan project review by BEA, BPC, the EPC, or the owner. China-side factory configuration, labels, manuals, and test reports should be reviewed for Bhutan-specific language, altitude, protection settings, and 230/400 V grid parameters.
china_standards: NB/T 32004 - 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 - 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 19964-2024 - 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
rows.gap: Gap: A China domestic inverter dossier may omit Bhutan project details that matter in review, including BPC interconnection settings, 230/400 V firmware validation, high-altitude thermal derating, site-specific protection coordination, and owner-required IEC evidence. Prepare a Bhutan-specific technical submittal instead of relying on Chinese certification alone. For large or sensitive projects, confirm whether India-interconnection or owner procurement standards add additional ride-through, power-quality, metering, or SCADA requirements.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but normally mandatory in practice for BPC grid connection, BEA-regulated activities, and owner/EPC technical acceptance
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic approvals are not transferable to Bhutan project approval and do not prove Bhutan site suitability

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not rely on a China domestic PV inverter dossier as a complete Bhutan project submittal. Add BPC connection settings, Bhutan 230/400 V at 50 Hz firmware validation, high-altitude derating evidence, and the IEC reports requested by the owner or EPC. Confirm BEA and BPC review expectations before committing the inverter model to a Bhutan project.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence - Bhutan PV Inverter Projects
rows.target_requirement: For Bhutan solar PV inverter projects, IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the core international safety standards for power converters used in photovoltaic systems. BSB standards adoption, BEA/BPC technical review, EPC procurement, lender due diligence, or project-owner specifications may require IEC 62109 type-test certificates for the exact inverter model or model family. Because Bhutan includes high-altitude Himalayan sites, safety evidence should also be checked for altitude-related insulation coordination, thermal performance, enclosure protection, and derating assumptions.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 - Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems - Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 - Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems - Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
Bhutan Standards Bureau - national standards adoption and conformity-assessment context
Bhutan Electricity Authority and Bhutan Power Corporation - project-level technical review context
rows.china_equivalent: China-side inverter safety evidence commonly includes GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, domestic CQC or CGC voluntary certification, and CNAS laboratory reports. These can help explain product design and domestic compliance, but they are not automatically equivalent to IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence for Bhutan. A Chinese report should be checked for the exact model, component substitutions, firmware, enclosure rating, altitude limits, and whether the tested configuration matches the unit supplied to Bhutan.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 - 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 - 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: A China domestic GB/T or NB/T certificate is not a substitute for IEC 62109-1/-2 safety type-test evidence where Bhutan project documents call for IEC standards. Exporters should obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 certificates or reports from an accredited laboratory for the model family supplied, confirm report validity after any bill-of-materials or firmware changes, and document altitude derating for Bhutan sites. Manuals and labels should state installation limits, protective earthing, overvoltage category, enclosure rating, and ambient/altitude derating in terms acceptable to the project reviewer.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent; mandatory in practice where BSB-adopted standards, BEA/BPC review, lender requirements, owner specifications, or EPC procurement documents require IEC 62109 evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or project-driven in China for many inverter certification schemes; not automatically accepted as Bhutan IEC 62109 evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not assume GB/T 37408-2021 or NB/T 32004 safety evidence satisfies Bhutan IEC 62109 expectations. Obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 evidence for the supplied inverter model or family, check report coverage after design changes, and document high-altitude derating before BEA, BPC, owner, or EPC review.
